claim.context: Green Buildings Ordinance | Denver Code Ch. 10, Art. XIII | Applies to buildings ≥25,000 SF

verdicts.note: The official Denver government documents explicitly confirm that Denver Code Chapter 10, Article XIII contains the Green Buildings Ordinance and applies to buildings of 25,000 square feet or greater.

claim.text: Applies to buildings ≥25,000 SF; requires green roof OR solar panels (42% roof coverage) OR cool roof + energy reduction commitment
claim.context: Green Buildings Ordinance | Denver Code Ch. 10, Art. XIII | Applies to buildings ≥25,000 SF; requires green roof OR solar panels (42% roof coverage) OR cool roof + energy reduction commitment

verdicts.note: The claim misrepresents the ordinance structure by presenting "cool roof + energy reduction commitment" as one alternative option among three, when the cool roof is actually a mandatory baseline requirement for all projects, with additional compliance options selected separately.

claim.context: Denver Green Code | Based on IgCC 2022 | Mandatory for commercial/multifamily

verdicts.note: The Denver Green Code 2022 is based on the International Green Construction Code 2021 (IGCC 2021), not IgCC 2022.

claim.context: Solar-Ready Zone | 2022 Denver Energy Code | Commercial buildings: 60% of roof area must be solar-ready zone

verdicts.note: The 2022 Denver Energy Code exists and explicitly requires commercial buildings to have a solar-ready zone covering not less than 60% of roof area.

claim.text: 60% of roof area must be solar-ready zone
claim.context: Commercial buildings: 60% of roof area must be solar-ready zone; pathways for conduit routing required

verdicts.note: The 60% solar-ready zone requirement for commercial buildings is explicitly stated in Denver's Energy Code (2021 and draft 2025 versions), and the conduit routing pathway requirement is also confirmed in the authoritative code sections.

claim.context: Electric-Ready | State requirement (effective 2023) | EV charging infrastructure preparation required

verdicts.note: ** The requirement is a Denver local building code requirement (effective May 1, 2023), not a state requirement as claimed in the report.

claim.context: Energy Benchmarking | Energize Denver Policy | Buildings ≥25,000 SF must submit annual benchmarking data

verdicts.note: The Energize Denver Building Performance Policy is an official Denver ordinance that requires buildings 25,000 sq. ft. and larger to meet energy-saving goals including annual benchmarking data submission.

claim.text: Buildings ≥25,000 SF must submit annual benchmarking data and meet EUI targets
claim.context: Energy Benchmarking | Energize Denver Policy | Buildings ≥25,000 SF must submit annual benchmarking data and meet EUI targets

verdicts.note: Multiple authoritative sources, including official Denver government documents and technical guidance, explicitly confirm that buildings 25,000 SF and larger must submit annual benchmarking data and meet EUI targets under the Energize Denver Policy.

claim.text: Mixed-use with residential component must pursue full WELL Certification (not WELL Core)
claim.context: Note: Mixed-use with residential component must pursue full WELL Certification (not WELL Core).

verdicts.note: No Denver building code section or policy document on denvergov.org explicitly states this requirement; while WELL Certification standards exist, this specific Denver mandate cannot be verified through available authoritative sources.

claim.text: Design to 20% better than ASHRAE 90.1-2019 baseline
claim.context: Energy Performance: Design to 20% better than ASHRAE 90.1-2019 baseline

verdicts.note: While "20% better than ASHRAE 90.1-2019" is a valid performance metric format used in Denver's energy code framework, the search results do not confirm this as a specific mandatory requirement or standard designation in Denver's building code.

claim.context: Design to 20% better than ASHRAE 90.1-2019 baseline

verdicts.note: ASHRAE 90.1-2019 is explicitly confirmed as an authoritative baseline standard in Denver's 2022 Energy Code documents and is actively used for building performance compliance.

claim.text: 42% of roof area for solar PV coverage
claim.context: Install solar PV covering 42% of roof area to satisfy Green Buildings Ordinance

verdicts.note: The 42% of roof area solar PV coverage requirement is explicitly stated in Denver's official Green Buildings Ordinance Rules and Regulations as one of the compliance options for on-site solar panels.
